steps.title: Eliminate the common term using addition
steps.content:
The equations both contain the term $5(y-14)$ with opposite signs: it appears as $+5(y-14)$ in the first equation and $-5(y-14)$ in the second. This makes them perfect for elimination. Adding the two equations together:

$$\left[\frac{x}{7}+5(y-14)\right]+\left[\frac{x}{3}-5(y-14)\right]=29+41$$

The $5(y-14)$ terms cancel, leaving:

$$\frac{x}{7}+\frac{x}{3}=70$$

steps.title: Combine fractions and solve for 10x
steps.content:
To add the fractions on the left, find the least common denominator of 7 and 3, which is 21:

$$\frac{3x}{21}+\frac{7x}{21}=70$$

$$\frac{10x}{21}=70$$

Multiply both sides by 21 to isolate $10x$:

$$10x = 70 \times 21 = 1470$$
